title: Add stack catalog and list selection helper
description: Introduce a canonical `STACKS` catalog in `src/agent_takt/onboarding/prompts.py` and add `_select_from_list()` with stream-injection support, default selection display, integer parsing, and retry-on-invalid behavior.
acceptance_criteria: `STACKS` is defined in `prompts.py` with the supported display names and default commands from the spec.
`_select_from_list()` returns a 0-based index and supports injected input/output streams.
Invalid non-integer and out-of-range selections produce an error and re-prompt instead of crashing.
The helper can be reused by both runner and stack prompts.

handoff_summary.completed: Added module-level `STACKS: list[tuple[str, str, str]]` with seven entries (Python, Node.js, TypeScript, Go, Rust, Java Maven, Other). Added `_select_from_list(prompt_text, options, default_index=0, *, stream_in, stream_out) -> int` that prints a numbered 1-based menu, accepts an empty line as the default, re-prompts with a clear error on non-integer input, re-prompts on out-of-range integer, and returns a 0-based index. File passes `python -m py_compile`.
handoff_summary.remaining: Downstream beads (not this one): (1) rewrite `collect_init_answers()` to call `_select_from_list` for runner and stack selection; (2) tighten `scaffold.py:_language_specific_known_issues()` to use canonical names; (3) fix `init.py` non-interactive defaults to reference `STACKS[0]`; (4) add/update tests.
handoff_summary.risks: None — no existing callers were changed. The new symbols are purely additive.
handoff_summary.next_action: Tester should verify: (a) `_select_from_list` returns the correct 0-based index for a valid integer, (b) empty input returns `default_index`, (c) non-integer input triggers an error message and re-prompt (no exception), (d) out-of-range integer triggers an error and re-prompt, (e) `STACKS[0]` is Python with expected commands, (f) `STACKS[-1]` is Other with empty command strings.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: None. Only `prompts.py` was modified; sibling beads touching `scaffold.py` and `init.py` operate on different files.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: Used a simple numbered 1-based menu with a `while True` retry loop — no curses or ANSI, consistent with the existing `_prompt()` stream-injection model. `default_index` is stored and displayed as 0-based internally but shown as 1-based to the user to match standard numbered-list conventions. Out-of-range and non-integer errors print a concrete range hint so the user knows valid values without re-reading the menu.
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Test `_select_from_list` with: (1) valid in-range integer → correct 0-based return; (2) empty input → `default_index` returned; (3) non-integer string → error written to stream, then valid input accepted; (4) integer below 1 or above len(options) → error written, then valid input accepted; (5) `default_index` at non-zero position; (6) `STACKS` contents spot-check (Python at index 0, Other at index -1 with empty strings).
handoff_summary.known_limitations: `collect_init_answers()` still uses the old free-text runner loop and the old `_prompt()` for language — that integration is intentionally deferred to the next bead in this feature tree.

conflict_risks: None. Only `prompts.py` was modified; sibling beads touching `scaffold.py` and `init.py` operate on different files.

execution_history.summary: Added `STACKS` catalog and `_select_from_list()` helper to `src/agent_takt/onboarding/prompts.py`. Both additions are in scope for this bead; `collect_init_answers()` is left unchanged pending the next bead that wires these into the prompt flow.
